topics.name: 稀缺性与机会成本
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 数学建模与应用题审题 before 稀缺性与机会成本; it provides the foundation used by later steps.

related.reason: Opportunity cost is about the next best forgone alternative, not about nominal versus real measurement.
Opportunity cost is a core input when comparing costs and benefits of choices.
The production possibilities frontier visualizes scarcity, trade-offs, and opportunity cost.
稀缺性与机会成本 and 数学建模与应用题审题 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 生产可能性边界 after 稀缺性与机会成本; it is the next topic in this course sequence.

typical_misconceptions: 脱离模型假设直接套结论
把相关关系当成因果关系
忽略机会成本、边际变化或外部性

区分短期长期、个体整体和相关因果

topics.name: 生产可能性边界
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 稀缺性与机会成本 before 生产可能性边界; it provides the foundation used by later steps.

related.reason: 生产可能性边界 and 稀缺性与机会成本 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 需求供给模型 after 生产可能性边界; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
After using the PPF to identify scarcity and trade-offs, learners can analyze how markets allocate goods through demand and supply.
PPF questions apply scarcity and opportunity cost by showing feasible choices and the cost of moving along the frontier.
Students often confuse movement along a fixed PPF with outward shifts caused by growth or productivity improvements.

topics.name: 需求供给模型
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 生产可能性边界 before 需求供给模型; it provides the foundation used by later steps.
Function concepts are a math prerequisite for reading demand and supply curves as mappings between price and quantity.
Demand-supply analysis needs graph-reading skill before learners interpret curve shifts, slopes, and intersections on price-quantity axes.

related.reason: Micro demand-supply curves differ from macro aggregate demand and aggregate supply models in scope and interpretation.
Demand curves connect individual choice to market demand and equilibrium outcomes.
After drawing demand and supply curves, elasticity helps explain response size and incidence.
Linear function graph skills transfer directly to drawing demand and supply schedules as curves on price-quantity axes.
Set up price as an input and quantity as an output before solving shifts, intersections, and equilibrium changes.
Slope and intercept describe a line, while economic interpretation asks whether the curve is demand or supply and what changed.
Supply and demand curves apply function modeling by translating economic assumptions into price-quantity relationships.
Finding market equilibrium follows the same procedural step as locating the intersection of two linear functions.
A curve's mathematical monotonicity is not the same as the economic cause of a demand or supply shift.
Supply and demand analysis applies the function concept by treating price and quantity as linked variables.
Linear demand and supply schedules apply linear function expressions when learners write equations for market curves.
Market equilibrium applies linear system solving when demand and supply equations are set equal.
Drawing supply and demand curves proceeds through coordinate-system setup with quantity and price axes.
需求供给模型 and 生产可能性边界 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 弹性分析 after 需求供给模型; it is the next topic in this course sequence.

typical_misconceptions: 脱离模型假设直接套结论
把相关关系当成因果关系
忽略机会成本、边际变化或外部性
skills: 识别模型假设、变量和约束条件
用图像、公式或边际分析解释经济机制
区分短期长期、个体整体和相关因果

topics.name: 弹性分析
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 需求供给模型 before 弹性分析; it provides the foundation used by later steps.
Slope and intercept ideas prepare learners to compare absolute curve changes with percentage ratio changes in elasticity.
Elasticity compares percentage changes as a ratio, so learners need ratio meaning before interpreting elastic and inelastic responses.

related.reason: 弹性分析 and 需求供给模型 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 消费者选择 after 弹性分析; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Elasticity analysis starts by identifying the relevant demand or supply curve and the direction of price or quantity change.
Elasticity supports cost-benefit reasoning by estimating how consumers or firms respond to price and policy changes.
Point elasticity applies derivative calculation by multiplying the local rate of quantity change by the price-quantity ratio.
Elasticity work first identifies the function linking price and quantity, then compares percentage changes along that relation.
The slope of a demand curve is an absolute rate, while elasticity is a percentage ratio change and varies with the point.
Elasticity applies percentage-change reasoning from growth-rate problems to price and quantity responses.
Point elasticity proceeds from average percentage changes to local rate-of-change reasoning expressed by the derivative definition.
Elasticity is a ratio of percentage changes, not a claim that price and quantity are directly proportional.
Point elasticity applies derivative concepts to describe local responsiveness of quantity to price.
Elasticity applies percent meaning because it compares percentage changes in price and quantity.
Point elasticity applies tangent-slope reasoning when interpreting the local slope of a demand curve.
Advanced elasticity work can proceed to chain-rule reasoning when variables affect demand through intermediate functions.
Elasticity measures responsiveness, while inflation measures changes in the overall price level; both use percentages but answer different questions.

topics.name: 消费者选择
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 弹性分析 before 消费者选择; it provides the foundation used by later steps.

related.reason: 消费者选择 and 弹性分析 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 边际效用递减 after 消费者选择; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Consumer choice problems proceed from preferences and budget constraints to marginal utility comparisons at the chosen bundle.
Consumer choice explains where individual demand curves come from before they are aggregated into market demand.
A budget constraint is a specific income-price limit, while opportunity cost is the broader value of the next best forgone option.

topics.name: 边际效用递减
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 消费者选择 before 边际效用递减; it provides the foundation used by later steps.

related.reason: 边际效用递减 and 消费者选择 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 短期成本曲线 after 边际效用递减; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Marginal utility calculations feed into consumer choice by comparing marginal utility per unit of price across goods.
Diminishing marginal utility helps explain why demand curves usually slope downward.
Marginal utility describes added satisfaction from consumption, while elasticity describes responsiveness to price or income changes.

topics.name: 短期成本曲线
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 边际效用递减 before 短期成本曲线; it provides the foundation used by later steps.
Derivative calculation is a math prerequisite for interpreting marginal cost as the rate of change of total cost.
Marginal cost depends on understanding a derivative as the instantaneous change in total cost with respect to output.

related.reason: 短期成本曲线 and 边际效用递减 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 厂商利润最大化 after 短期成本曲线; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Short-run cost curves are used next to compare marginal revenue with marginal cost and determine profit-maximizing output.
Perfect competition problems apply short-run cost curves to shutdown, supply, and profit calculations.
Monotonicity and extrema analysis helps explain turning points and marginal-average relationships on cost curves.
Short-run cost curves apply derivative calculation when deriving marginal cost from a total cost function.
Average and marginal cost curves apply function graph analysis to compare shape, turning points, and intersections.
Explaining cost-curve curvature can proceed to second-derivative tests for convexity and minimum-cost points.
Marginal cost applies the derivative definition as the limiting change in total cost per extra unit of output.
Marginal cost applies tangent-line reasoning when a cost curve is approximated near a chosen output level.
Cost-curve shape applies concavity and inflection reasoning when interpreting increasing or decreasing marginal cost.
Short-run production cost concepts are narrower than general cost-benefit analysis and depend on fixed versus variable inputs.

typical_misconceptions: 脱离模型假设直接套结论
把相关关系当成因果关系
忽略机会成本、边际变化或外部性

topics.name: 厂商利润最大化
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 短期成本曲线 before 厂商利润最大化; it provides the foundation used by later steps.
Absolute extrema reasoning is a math prerequisite for checking whether a candidate output maximizes profit over the feasible range.

related.reason: 厂商利润最大化 and 短期成本曲线 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 完全竞争市场 after 厂商利润最大化; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Profit maximization requires identifying revenue, total cost, marginal cost, and marginal revenue before choosing output.
Profit maximization applies derivatives because marginal revenue and marginal cost are rates of change of revenue and cost.
After setting marginal revenue equal to marginal cost, learners test candidate extreme points against boundaries and assumptions.
Firm profit maximization is an economics application of optimization: choose output to maximize profit under feasibility constraints.
Profit maximization applies monotonicity and extrema reasoning when comparing marginal revenue, marginal cost, and boundary outputs.
After MR equals MC, the second-derivative test helps check whether the candidate output is a profit maximum.
Profit maximization applies critical-point analysis when MR equals MC or a boundary output must be checked.
Profit maximization applies absolute-extrema reasoning because the best output must be chosen over the feasible range.
Firm output choice applies derivative-extrema methods to locate and verify profit peaks.
Constrained profit problems apply Lagrange multiplier ideas when firms optimize under resource or technology constraints.
Firm profit rules are applied directly when analyzing output and entry decisions in competitive markets.
Economic profit includes opportunity cost, while simple accounting comparisons may omit implicit costs.

topics.name: 完全竞争市场
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 厂商利润最大化 before 完全竞争市场; it provides the foundation used by later steps.

related.reason: 完全竞争市场 and 厂商利润最大化 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 垄断市场 after 完全竞争市场; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Competitive-market analysis proceeds by applying the firm profit rule under the price-taking assumption.
Perfect competition applies demand and supply logic to markets with many buyers and sellers and no individual price control.
Perfect competition and monopoly use different assumptions about price-taking, market power, and marginal revenue.

topics.name: 垄断市场
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 完全竞争市场 before 垄断市场; it provides the foundation used by later steps.

related.reason: 垄断市场 and 完全竞争市场 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 外部性与矫正政策 after 垄断市场; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Monopoly problems use the profit-maximization workflow but require a separate marginal revenue curve below demand.
Monopoly is a market power case that can be compared with other sources of market failure such as externalities.
Students often apply competitive price-taking rules to monopoly even though monopoly faces the market demand curve.

topics.name: 外部性与矫正政策
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master 垄断市场 before 外部性与矫正政策; it provides the foundation used by later steps.

related.reason: 外部性与矫正政策 and 垄断市场 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 公共物品与搭便车 after 外部性与矫正政策; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Externality analysis starts from market demand and supply, then adds private and social cost or benefit curves.
Externality cases are common applications for evaluating taxes, subsidies, regulation, and efficiency-equity trade-offs.
Externalities and public goods are both market failures, but public goods are defined by non-rivalry and non-excludability.

Practice GDP 核算 after 公共物品与搭便车; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
After identifying public-good features, learners compare provision, financing, and equity trade-offs.
Public goods create market failure, but the key test is non-excludability and non-rivalry rather than any external cost or benefit.
Public-good cases apply market-government boundary reasoning when judging whether collective provision is needed.

Practice CPI 与通货膨胀 after GDP 核算; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
GDP accounting problems proceed by reading economic data, identifying components, and applying the expenditure or income approach.
GDP data often require converting between nominal and real values before comparing output across time.
GDP deflator and CPI both measure price changes, but GDP accounting covers domestic production while CPI tracks a consumer basket.

topics.name: CPI 与通货膨胀
topics.subject: economics

prerequisites.reason: Master GDP 核算 before CPI 与通货膨胀; it provides the foundation used by later steps.

related.reason: CPI measures consumer price changes, while GDP accounting measures output and income flows.
Inflation indicators are central evidence for monetary policy decisions.
Inflation analysis requires converting nominal values to real values and reading price indexes correctly.
CPI and inflation calculations apply percent-change methods to compare price levels across periods.
CPI applies weighted-mean reasoning because category prices contribute according to basket weights.
Inflation time-series work applies line-chart trend reading to describe changes in the price level.
Inflation-rate questions apply growth-rate problem solving when comparing CPI values between periods.
CPI 与通货膨胀 and GDP 核算 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 失业类型 after CPI 与通货膨胀; it is the next topic in this course sequence.

Unemployment-rate series apply line-chart trend reading when learners compare peaks, troughs, and recoveries.

Aggregate demand and aggregate supply analysis applies function graph reading to interpret shifts, intersections, and output-price changes.
AD-AS diagrams apply linear function graph skills when learners compare slopes, shifts, and intercept-style readings.
Short-run macro equilibrium applies intersection reasoning where aggregate demand and aggregate supply meet.
Aggregate demand and supply apply function modeling by translating macro assumptions into output-price relationships.
After finding AD-AS equilibrium, learners can trace how spending changes are amplified through aggregate demand.
Micro demand-supply explains one market, while AD-AS explains economy-wide output and the price level.
